description: Kubernetes utility commands for port-forwarding, debugging, and log tailing

commands.description: Start/stop/check port forwards to HyperFleet services in Kubernetes

executions.output:
Port Forward Status
  ● hyperfleet-api - localhost:8000 (PID: 1456)
  ● postgresql - localhost:5432 (PID: 1455)
  ● maestro-http - localhost:8100 (PID: 1476)
  ● maestro-grpc - localhost:8090 (PID: 1475)
executions.notes: Green ● = running with PID, red ● = not running

commands.description: Interactive selection of kubectl context and namespace
commands.notes: Interactive - requires user input, not recorded

commands.description: Execute curl from an ephemeral pod inside the cluster
behavior: Creates or reuses a curl pod in the cluster
Supports -H headers, -d data, -F file upload, -v verbose
Useful for testing in-cluster service connectivity

commands.description: Create debug pod from deployment template
behavior: Creates a pod with same spec but restartPolicy=Never
Execs into the pod for debugging

commands.description: Tail logs from pods matching a name pattern
behavior: If stern available and multiple pods match, uses stern for multi-pod tailing
Otherwise prompts user to select a pod
Passes through kubectl logs options (-f, --since, --tail, etc.)

commands.description: Tail adapter logs filtered by current cluster ID
behavior: Finds pods matching adapter name pattern
Filters log output to include time and message fields
Filters by cluster-id to show only relevant entries
